back_translation: You shall not sacrifice to the Lord your God an ox or a sheep in which there is a blemish, any bad thing, for an abomination of the Lord your God it is.

When there is found in your midst, in one of your gates that the Lord your God is giving to you, a man or a woman who does what is evil in the eyes of the Lord your God, by transgressing His covenant,
and he goes and serves other gods, and bows down to them, and to the sun or to the moon or to any host of the heavens, which I have not commanded,
and it is told to you and you hear, and you investigate thoroughly, and behold, truth, established the matter, this abomination has been done in Israel,
then you shall bring out that man or that woman who has done this evil thing to your gates, the man or the woman, and you shall stone them with stones and they shall die.
On the mouth of two witnesses or three witnesses shall the one who dies be put to death; he shall not be put to death on the mouth of one witness.
The hand of the witnesses shall be against him first to put him to death, and the hand of all the people last. And you shall purge the evil from your midst.

When a matter is too difficult for you for judgment, between blood and blood, between case and case, and between assault and assault, words of disputes in your gates, then you shall arise and go up to the place that the Lord your God will choose in it.
And you shall come to the Levitical priests and to the judge who shall be in those days, and you shall inquire, and they shall tell you the matter of the judgment.
And you shall do according to the word that they shall tell you, from that place that the Lord will choose, and you shall keep to do according to all that they shall instruct you.

According to the mouth of the law that they shall instruct you, and according to the judgment that they shall say to you, you shall do; you shall not turn aside from the word that they shall tell you, right or left.
And the man who acts with arrogance, not listening to the priest who stands to serve there the Lord your God, or to the judge, that man shall die, and you shall purge the evil from Israel.
And all the people shall hear and fear, and shall not act presumptuously again.
When you come to the land that the Lord your God is giving to you, and you possess it and dwell in it, and you say: Let me set over me a king like all the nations that are around me,

setting you shall set over yourself a king whom the Lord your God will choose in him; from the midst of your brothers you shall set over yourself a king; you shall not be able to give over yourself a foreign man who is not your brother.

Only he shall not multiply for himself horses, and he shall not cause the people to return to Egypt so as to multiply horses, for the Lord has said to you: You shall not continue to return on this way again.
And he shall not multiply for himself wives, lest his heart turn aside, and silver and gold he shall not multiply for himself exceedingly.
And it shall be, when he sits on the throne of his kingdom, he shall write for himself a copy of this law on a scroll from before the Levitical priests.
And it shall be with him, and he shall read in it all the days of his life, so that he may learn to fear the Lord his God, to keep all the words of this law and these statutes, to do them,

so that his heart shall not be lifted up above his brothers, and so that he shall not turn aside from the commandment, right or left, in order that he may prolong days upon his kingdom, he and his sons, in the midst of Israel.
